title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Distressed by the realization that a woman was murdered eighteen years ago, Gwendolyn is comforted by Jiles. Miss Marple arrives at the manor and joins Gwendolyn, Jiles, and Mrs. Coleridge, asserting that recent events confirm Helen Harley was indeed murdered at a young age.

Miss Marple proposes that Helen's body is not in the basement, as Edith Pettigrew had previously claimed, but is instead buried at the end of the terrace. She explains that the relocation of the garden steps provided a pretext for digging that would avoid suspicion from the gardener. Miss Marple notes that Lily Kimball’s letter mentioned a Swiss girl named Leonie, who witnessed something suspicious from a first-floor window—likely the digging of a pit—but Leonie never reported it.

Jiles reconstructs the crime, suggesting the killer strangled Helen in the hall, moved her to a bed, and waited for Kelvin Harley to pass out from drugged whiskey before transporting the body to the terrace for burial. This theory implies the killer remained lurking near the house throughout the night. Miss Marple advises the group to rely on confirmed facts rather than anyone's word. Consequently, Jiles begins re-examining the suspects, specifically questioning the testimony of Erskine. While Gwendolyn believes Erskine has been ruled out, Jiles suggests that Erskine's claim of a mutual parting from Helen might be a lie intended to hide a motive of obsessive love and potential threats.
